title: India likely to maintain Russian oil purchases
section_path: India likely to maintain Russian oil purchases
section_path_text: India likely to maintain Russian oil purchases
summary:
Main
Russian oil arrivals to India to sustain amid new tariffs from the US and current Middle East conflict. This will lower demand for mainstream crude. By Ivan Mathews
India's crude and condensate imports reached a record 5.3mbd in February.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Russian oil arrivals to India to sustain amid new tariffs from the US and current Middle East conflict. This will lower demand for mainstream crude.

children.title: New tariff rates will lead to continued purchases of Russian crude
section_path: New tariff rates will lead to continued purchases of Russian crude
children.section_path_text: New tariff rates will lead to continued purchases of Russian crude
children.summary: Before the US Supreme Court decision, India and the US agreed on February 2 to an interim framework that would cap US tariffs on Indian goods at 18%. However, the US Supreme Court on February 20 struck down President Trump's wide-ranging tariffs on foreign imports.

children.title: Venezuelan crude purchases lower India's demand for mainstream crude
section_path: Venezuelan crude purchases lower India's demand for mainstream crude
children.section_path_text: Venezuelan crude purchases lower India's demand for mainstream crude
children.summary: Reliance has acquired roughly 4mb Venezuelan crude from Chevron and Vitol, and received a general licence to import Venezuelan oil. An additional 4mb Venezuelan crude have been sold to IOC and HPCL for April delivery (sources: Reuters and Argus).

children.title: Ongoing conflict could lead to delayed arrivals of Middle East crude into India
section_path: Ongoing conflict could lead to delayed arrivals of Middle East crude into India
children.section_path_text: Ongoing conflict could lead to delayed arrivals of Middle East crude into India
children.summary: Cargoes loaded from within Straits of Hormuz and bound for India could face delivery delays amid the ongoing Middle East conflict. In February 2026, India received 2.8mbd crude from the region, accounting for 53% of total imports.
